sections.paragraph:
fut. יִזַּל-

(1) to flow, to run, Num 24:7 Psa 147:18. Part. pl. נֹוזְלִים fluids, poet. used of streams, Exo 15:8 Isa 44:3 Jer 18:14 Psa 78:16 Pro 5:15. Figuratively applied to speech, Deu 32:2, “my speech shall flow like dew;” used of a sweet odour pervading the air, Son 4:16.-Like other verbs of flowing (see הָלַךְ No. 4 , Heb. Gram. § 135, note 1)-

(2) it is construed with an acc. of whatever flows down plentifully, Jer 9:17, וְעַפְעַפֵּינוּ יִזְּלוּ־מַיִם “and our eyelids flow down with water;” Isa 45:8 Job 36:28.

(3) From the Arabic usage (نزل), to descend; also to turn aside to lodge, to dwell; whence מַזָּלֹות.-Note נָֽזְלוּ Jud. 5:5 , is for נָזֹלּוּ Niphal, from זָלַל, which see.

Hiphil הִזִּיל causat. of No. 1, to cause to flow, Isa 48:21. The same form is found under זָלַל.
